summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates installer identity (OS username, git name/email) to hardcoded remote server
details: On `npm install`, dist/scripts/preinstall.js executes automatically via the package.json preinstall lifecycle hook and collects installer-side identity data: `os.userInfo().username`, plus `user.name` and `user.email` parsed from the installer's `~/.gitconfig` via iniparser. These three values are transmitted as query-string parameters to a hardcoded HTTPS endpoint at `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext`. The data collection is not disclosed in README.md/README.txt and there is no opt-out. Git-configured email is commonly the installer's corporate or personal identity on GitHub and similar systems, i.e. PII harvested without consent on every install. A separate postinstall script rewrites a package.json resolved via `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../package.json')`, stripping scripts/devDependencies/dependencies — intended to target the package's own manifest but fragile under non-standard install layouts (secondary quality concern). The exfiltration alone is sufficient: undocumented installer-identity collection to an author-controlled endpoint at install time is an active-attack pattern regardless of whether the author considers it benign telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
